title: Research Projects
text: Cholesterol and matrisome pathways dysregulated in astrocytes and microglia The impact of Apolipoprotein E ε4 (APOE4), the strongest genetic risk factor for Alzheimer’s disease (AD), on human brain cellular function remains unclear. Here we investigated the effects of APOE4 on brain cell types derived from population and isogenic human induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs), post-mortem brain and APOE targeted replacement (APOE-TR) mice. Population and isogenic models demonstrate that APOE4 local haplotype rather...

title: Lab Members
text: Principal Investigator Julia TCW, Ph.D. Julia TCW is a Principal Investigator at the TCW laboratory at Boston University (primary appointment at Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics). She received a Ph.D. and A.M. in Molecular and Cellular Biology from Harvard University with a Biophysics training background and research studies in induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) reprogramming in the Department of Stem Cell and Regenerative Biology. She then perused her postdoctoral research in the Department of Neurosci...

title: Research News
text: Do Lipids Lubricate ApoE's Part in Alzheimer Mechanisms? 05 November 2021, Alzforum APOE4 Glia Fumble Lipid Handling APOE is an apolipoprotein, tasked with ferrying cholesterol and other lipids between cells. In the brain, it is produced mainly by astrocytes and activated microglia. Recent work by Julia TCW and Alison Goate at Icahn School of Medicine, Mount Sinai, New York, found the E4 allele disrupts the lipid balance in astrocytes and microglia derived from human iPSCs, boosting cholesterol production while hob...

title: Rufuto’s Graduation
text: Rufuto Rahman, wet lab PhD student that specialized in CRISPR-Cas9 based gene editing, has graduated. Congratulations!! It has been a pleasure to have Rufuto as a team member and we will miss him.

title: Juao-Guilherme Rosa won the Trainee Professional Development Award
text: Juao's exciting work has been recognized by the Society for Neuroscience (SfN) for scientific excellence and has won the travel grant for attending Neuroscience 2024 in Chicago. Congratulations

title: Deepti Graduation
text: Deepti Murthy, a diligent dry lab student that specialized in single-cell sequencing analysis, has graduated from college and departs for San Diego to pursue a Ph.D. in bioinformatics. It has been a pleasure to have Deepti as a member of the lab and we all look forward to witness her exciting work in the future.

title: Lu Qian is selected in the Neurophotonics Research Traineeship Program (NRT)
text: PhD student Lu Qian was selected as a trainee in the Neurophotonics Research Traineeship Program (NRT), which is a university-wide community with focused professional development and graduate training in neurophotonics . Congratulations!

title: Swazi Graduation
text: Swazi Sharma is graduating from BU and we are all excited to congratulate her as she embarks on a new chapter at Brigham Women's Hospital.

title: Juao becomes recipient of T32 Grant!
text: PhD Student Juao-Guilherme Rosa was awarded the NIGMS T32 BioMolecular Pharmacology grant. We're truly excited for him and wish him for more such achievements in the future.

title: Celebrating our Cell publication!

title: Our newest publication is in Cell!
text: We are excited to share that “Cholesterol and matrisome pathways dysregulated in astrocytes and microglia” is published in Molecular Cell. Here, we used hiPSCs, post-mortem human brain and mouse models to study about the strongest AD risk factor, APOE4. Using these models, Dr. TCW and colleagues found that there are more than just APOE4 mutation, which is APOE nearby genetic region called haplotype to increase disease risk, and the mechanistic defects due to APOE4 are unique to human cells. Furthermore, human glial...
